hook.text: Work hard in school and you'll succeed. It's the promise every society makes. Does it hold up?

buildup.text: Meritocracy claims that education rewards talent and effort, sorting people into positions they deserve.

discovery.text: If true, education is the great equalizer — giving everyone a fair shot regardless of background.

twist.text: Data shows parental income predicts educational success better than test scores or effort alone.

climax.text: The meritocratic ideal hides structural advantages. It makes inequality feel earned rather than inherited.

punchline.text: Merit exists — but the starting line isn't equal.

quiz.question: What does research say about meritocracy in education?
options: Parental income predicts success more than effort
